channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Cointelegraph - Security, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading, Blockthreat, CryptoPotato, CryptoSlate, Krebs On Security, The Hacker News, Coindesk, GroupIB Blog, Data Breach Today, CNN.com - Crypto News, DataBreaches.net

item.title: Kaspersky Lab experts have discovered a new attack vector and toolkit for compromising corporate Gmail accounts
item.description: Kaspersky Labs writes: It is used by the ToddyCat group. Kaspersky Lab experts have discovered a new attack vector and toolkit for compromising corporate Gmail accounts. Using this toolkit, attackers ...
item.dc:creator: DataBreaches.net

item.title: Langflow RCE Exploited to Deploy Monero Miner on Exposed AI App Endpoints
item.description:
Threat actors are continuing to exploit a critical Langflow vulnerability as part of fresh attacks designed to deliver a Monero cryptocurrency miner.
The activity has been found to weaponize CVE-2026...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Silent Swap Crypto Clipper Uses Fake Google Notes Extension to Replace Wallet Addresses
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged an active browser extension campaign that is designed to steal cryptocurrency by stealthily replacing wallet addresses when unsuspecting users initiate a transac...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: MiCA Deadline: New Rules Could Force 80% of Crypto Firms Out of EU
item.description: Investors are being urged to verify whether their crypto provider appears on the official MiCA register before using its services....
item.dc:creator: CryptoPotato

item.title: Ethereum Price Analysis: ETH Defends $1.5K Support, But Weak Demand Puts Recovery in Question
item.description: Ethereum continues to trade within a firmly bearish market structure despite showing signs of stabilization around a major support zone. While buyers have managed to defend the recent lows, both the d...
item.dc:creator: CryptoPotato

item.title: The Human Element: Building A Trusted Workforce in the Age of DPRK Employment Fraud
item.description: From Nisos: Earlier this year, our DPRK employment fraud investigation revealed how North Korean operatives infiltrate US companies at industrial scale. In June, we released Part 2 of our research, fe...
item.dc:creator: DataBreaches.net

item.title: GuardFall Exposes Open-Source AI Coding Agents to Decades-Old Shell Injection Risks
item.description:
The safety check that is supposed to stop an AI coding agent from running a dangerous command can be walked straight past using a shell trick that has been public for decades.
New research from Adver...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Pi Network (PI) Crashes to a New ATL: Going to Zero or Rebound Ahead?
item.description: PI has plummeted by over 96% since its historic peak of $3 witnessed at the start of 2025....
item.dc:creator: CryptoPotato

item.title: The Fall of XSS Forum: From DaMaGeLaB to the 2025 takedown
item.description: Ransomnews has published a history and analysis of XSS Forum from its inception to its seizure in 2025. There is so much that is interesting and informative in their report that it’s hard to know wha...
item.dc:creator: DataBreaches.net

item.title: 282 iOS AI Apps Leak API Keys and Open AI Proxy Access in Network Traffic Study
item.description:
Researchers tested 444 AI chatbot apps for iPhone and found that 282 of them, nearly two-thirds, exposed paid AI access through their network traffic.
In many cases, the path in was visible just by w...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News
